title: Hybrid Recommender Cold-Start Benchmark
summary: Build a warm-plus-cold hybrid recommender on Linux, using temporal splitting, metadata/embedding cold-start ranking, and a combined output contract.
taskPrompt: Use the staged interaction, metadata, embedding, and user-profile files to build a hybrid recommender. Materialize the staged runtime from `input/runtime_env/` with the provided software wrappers, then write `predictions_warm.csv`, `predictions_cold.csv`, `predictions_combined.csv`, `evaluation_report.json`, and `model_config.json` under `output/`.
agentMustDo: Build a warm-item recommender that uses both `rating` and `watch_percentage`.
Build a cold-start model from `item_metadata.csv` and `item_embeddings.json` without using cold-item interactions.
Use the canonical per-user temporal split: `70%` train, `10%` validation, `20%` test.
Fuse warm and cold scores into one combined ranked recommendation surface.
Write the five required deliverables under `output/`.

inputFiles.name: interactions.csv
inputFiles.description: Warm-item user interaction history with rating, watch percentage, and timestamps.

inputFiles.name: item_metadata.csv
inputFiles.description: Item features plus the `is_cold_start` label that defines warm vs. cold membership.

inputFiles.name: item_embeddings.json
inputFiles.description: Dense item embeddings used by the cold-start model.

inputFiles.name: user_profiles.csv
inputFiles.description: Per-user profile attributes available at solve time.

inputFiles.name: runtime_env
inputFiles.description: Pinned task-local `uv` manifest and lockfile for the agent-visible Python runtime.

referenceFiles.name: predictions_combined.csv
referenceFiles.description: Combined warm-plus-cold ranking with truthful `item_type` labels.

referenceFiles.name: evaluation_report.json
referenceFiles.description: Reported warm, cold, and combined evaluation summary.

referenceFiles.name: model_config.json
referenceFiles.description: Warm/cold model description, hybrid method, and split ratios.

evaluation:
Additive score in `1/6` increments from `0.0` to `1.0`.

Checks:
1. output completeness and exact schema checks for all five required files
2. split-contract correctness via documented temporal ratios, no cold items in warm predictions, and warm-item report metrics that stay broadly consistent with canonical recomputation
3. warm metrics: `NDCG@10 >= 0.03`, `HitRate@10 >= 0.15`, `RMSE <= 5.0`
4. cold metrics: `NDCG@10 >= 0.05`, `Coverage@10 >= 0.40`
5. hybrid integration: both warm and cold items must appear in the combined ranking, `item_type` must be truthful, and per-user scores must be monotonic
6. model documentation completeness for warm model, cold model, hybrid method, dimensionality parameter, and split ratios

Documented replay surface: positive fixture scores `1.0`, negative fixture scores `0.0`.
reservation: The raw submission metadata lists PyTorch 2.0, but the normalized benchmark runtime currently targets a lighter scikit-learn / SciPy baseline unless later stages prove torch is required.
